item.title: Spy Maters Camp II
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the link to book a spot!

item.title: Global Soccer Game Webinar
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Virtually via zoom, This event will be recorded and distributed after the event to all those who register., Tukwila, WA

Join Us for the FIFA Ready Webinar Date: February 10th, 2:30 PM Location: Virtually Via Zoom Event Overview: As we gear up for the monumental FIFA events, it&'s crucial to align our regional efforts to maximize economic and infrastructural benefits. This meeting will gather key stakeholders from various sectors, including our cities and business community, to strategize and synchronize our readiness plans with an emphasis in preparing our business community to ensure you are ready for this economic opportunity. So do you want to learn about what resources are available to you to maximize this time? Register to learn more now. These quarterly events are brought to you by the Seattle Southside Chamber of Commerce and Explore Seattle Southside. This meeting is a pivotal opportunity for stakeholders to collaborate and ensure our region is fully prepared to host FIFA events successfully. Together, we can create a unified and effective execution plan that benefits all.

item.title: Audubon Bird Walk
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Learn more about the birds at the Bellevue Botanical Garden. Join Eastside Audubon for a monthly walk that explores the birds and their habitat in the Garden. This one-mile walk will be on trails with various surfaces and may include gravel, mulch, or pavement. Please dress for the weather and wear comfortable walking shoes. Parking in the lot is free. From the parking lot enter the garden through the breezeway and turn left. Meet your guide in the covered Gathering Place. This walk is for any level of birder and no experience is necessary. Bring binoculars if you have them. There will be some available to loan for the walk. Each walk is $5 per person. Registration is required. Sorry, the June walk is full. View our calendar to register for upcoming walks.

From SEAtoday - Sports & Outdoors

item.title: Earth Month Event | Nature Journaling
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 10 AM

Nature Journaling in Pioneer Park It's Earth Month! And to celebrate we're hosting the first of our seasonal Nature Journaling educa tional events. We will be exploring Pioneer Park with notebooks and pens m aking observations about what we see. Participants will get instructions o n how to begin nature journaling as well as a series of prompts to help gu ide your observations. What is nature journaling? It is a practice in which you observe nature and spend some time crafting notes whether that' s words, drawings, painting, quantitative data, etc. Click here to learn more: https://johnmuirlaws.com/nature-journaling-starting-growing / All ages welcome, no experience necessary. When: Thursday, April 16th, 10am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park NW, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dr ess for the weather in comfortable clothes. Please bring a notebook, and pens/pencils/water colors/crayons/etc Registration: https://volunteersig nup.org/J7KYK

item.title: Green Issaquah: Restoration Event at Park Pointe
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: SE Evans St, Issaquah, WA

Join the Issaquah Alps Trails Club and Green Issaquah on the third Saturday of each month for invasive species removal events at Park Pointe! We will be removing invasive species such as Himalayan blackberry bushes to prepare for our fall planting event. These invasive plants inhibit the growth of native plants, and removing them will ensure the longevity and health of our newest additions. This event is part of our ongoing efforts to help meet the Issaquah Climate Action Plan goal of 55% tree canopy cover by 2035. When and Where Date & Time: Saturday, April 18th, 2025, 9 AM - Noon Where: Park Pointe Where to Park Parking for Park Pointe is available along SE Evans Street, which is overflow for Issaquah High School and the parking for the Sportsman Club. Where to Meet We will be meeting at the end of the dirt road at the trailhead. What to Bring Bring your own gloves if you have them. There will be gloves available for those who don't have their own. Please dress in layers and in clothing you don't mind getting dirty. Closed-toed shoes are required. Long sleeves and pants, especially denim, are strongly recommended. Bring a full water bottle and snacks. Don't forget to bring your sunscreen! Point of Contact Anne Newcomb \(anne@annenewcomb.net\) and Matt LaBelle \(mattlabelle@hotmail.com\), your work party leaders.

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

item.title: Daybreak Star Community 5K Run and Walk
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th \(37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend, starting at 9:00 AM\) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center \(5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle, WA 98199\) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group, please email communications@unitedindians.org

Event details

item.title: Earth Day Forest Bathing in the Garden
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Taught by Deborah Wilk. Celebrate Earth Day with a mindful Forest Bathing walk in the vibrant spring garden. Slow down, breathe deep, and open your senses with wonder as you deepen your connection to nature. Step away from the stressors of the outside world, as we restore mind, body and spirit while honoring our interdependence with the living world that surrounds and holds us, fostering a sense of gratitude and stewardship for the planet we call Home. Deborah Wilk, psychotherapist and co-founder of Mindful in the Wild, will guide you in practices that integrate mindfulness meditation and shinrin-yoku \(Japanese Forest Bathing\). The walk is approximately one mile and will be on trails with various surfaces, including some gentle inclines down to the more forested areas of the garden.

Event details

item.title: Seattle Waterfront Tour
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Offsite, Bellevue, WA

Join us for a 90-minute walking tour of Seattle’s newest and boldest urban park, led by Richard Hartlage of Land Morphology, which provided planting design services for this expansive project. The park skims along Elliott Bay, spanning 20 acres and 26 blocks, and features 500 plant varieties and nearly 800 trees. Each planting zone tells a unique story rooted in local history and ecology and exemplifies how thoughtful planting design creates spaces that connect the community with nature, enhance biodiversity, and serve as models for sustainable urban development. Overlook Walk is populated with species native to Puget Sound’s coastal bluffs, and provide valuable shade, habitat and stormwater filtration. The Aquarium Rooftop’s native meadow landscape was designed with guidance from traditional ecological knowledge-keeper Valerie Segrest, an enrolled member of the Muckleshoot Indian Tribe. BBGS Member $35/Non-Member $50. Tour leader’s bio:  Richard Hartlage is the CEO and founding partner of the Seattle-based landscape architectural firm Land Morphology. His award-winning designs are emotive, immersive spaces that incorporate sophisticated horticulture and historical knowledge to heighten the human experience of the natural world. The firm’s portfolio includes the Leach Botanical Garden in Portland, the Herb and Vegetable Garden at the Brooklyn Botanical Garden, and the Chihuly Garden and Glass.

item.title: Issaquah Sustainability Fair
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Pickering Barn, 1730 10th Ave NW, Issaquah, WA

This free event, in partnership with the Farmers Market, is held each year in tandem with our Earth Month celebration. You’re invited to join us for a day packed with fun, education, and inspiration! Here are some 2026 Sustainability Fair highlights: -Pedal towards a brighter future: Join Trek’s Bicycle and Gerk’s Ski and Cycle for e-bike testing and prepare for the next round of e-bike rebates. Families can also demonstrate their bike safety skills by participating in the Issaquah Police Department Bike Rodeo. -Save materials from the landfill while looking good doing it! Pick through items in the free clothing section hosted by the Interact Club and the Community Clothing Closet. After picking up some new clothes, visit the Furniture Repair Bank booth to find your next household statement piece. -Explore the new frontier of EVs at the Touch-a-Truck area! Learn about the first EV side-loading waste hauler in Washington State and visit the Recology booth to hear about healthy, clean, waste-reducing products you can buy locally. -Connect with our natural world. Meet one of the many environmental nonprofits in Issaquah, take home a free tree, and participate in a local conservation program all in one place! -Become a leading climate household. Discuss how to make your household a climate leader and save money while doing it by connecting with equipment installers like All Climate Heating and Air Conditioning and Northwest Electrical and Solar. Learn more: bit.ly/IssySustainabilityFair

Event details

item.title: All in for Autism 5K Run/Walk
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Bellevue Downtown Park, 10278 NE 1st St, Bellevue, WA

All in for Autism 5K Run/Walk MSarwary Thu, 02/12/2026 - 15:04 47.612864389021 -122.20417933176 47.612864389021, -122.20417933176 April 26 2026 8:00 AM - 10:00 AM All Day Event Off Event Registration On Price $30.00 Email John@schwager.org Telephone 206-226-5728 Location Bellevue Downtown Park, 10201 NE 4th St, Bellevue, WA 98004 Description All in for Autism's cause is to raise awareness and funds to support children with Autism Spectrum Disorder \(ASD\), their families, and caregivers with the ultimate goal of raising their quality of life. Raising a child on the autism spectrum can be an exhausting and lonely roller coaster ride. Many families cannot afford to pay for the services that their autistic children need. Funds raised from the race will benefit Kindering, Athletes for Kids, and other Rotary projects that benefit children and their families. Join us for the 5K \(run or walk!\) and the 200m Kids' Dash. 5k Run/Walk course with fun for the whole family. Dogs welcome. They will have their own special bandana for the occasion. Kid's Run - bring the kiddos for a full family event. Register Online The All in for Autism 5K , sponsored by Bellevue Breakfast Rotary Club, is included in the city calendar because a special event permit has been issued. Hide from City Calendar Off Canceled Off Location Bellevue Downtown Park

item.title: 3XBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Bush School, 3400 E Harrison St, Seattle, WA

Neighborhoods: Capitol Hill,Central Area/Squire Park,First Hill,Judkins Park,Madison Park,Madrona/Leschi,Montlake/Portage BayEvent Types: Community,Education,Hobbies,Holiday/Seasonal Events, Sports/Recreation,Youth Audience: Children,Special Needs,Teen S ponsoring Organization: 3x3 Basketball Association \(3XBA\) Contact: Lakin Roland Contact Email: info@3xba.com Pre-Register: Yes Cost: $250 \+ fees Registration Details: Two sessions offered each Sunday: Grades 2-5 from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM, Grades 6-8 from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM. The clinic runs for five Sundays: March 22, March 29, April 5, Apr il 26, and May 3, 2026 at The Bush School, Seattle. Early bird pricin g ends on March 1. Learn more about 3xBA and register at: https://www.3xba.com/register Link: https://3xba.ludus.com/index.php?show\_id=200517791 Help your athlete grow their game this spring with the 3xBA Spring 3x3 Clinic Series-a 5-week program combining fun, competit ive 3x3 basketball with small-group instruction led by 3xBA coaches. Players develop skills, confidence, and basketball IQ through structured play and hands-on coaching. Open to all experience levels for 2nd-8th graders. Athletes should bring a basketball and water bottle. Perfe ct for off-season development and team-style competition.

item.title: NPA Spring Plant Sale
item.description:
Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Bellevue Botanical Garden, 12001 Main St, Bellevue, WA

Join the NW Perennial Alliance for their Spring Plant Sale! April is a great time to buy plants from a select group of local small nurseries and specialty growers who sell an amazing collection of hard-to-find and old-time favorite perennials, shrubs, ferns, grasses and more. Your support helps these growers thrive and prosper, and in return they can grow even more special plants for our gardens! This is a ‘Farmer’s Market Style’ plant sale where you purchase plants directly from each seller. Bring your own small wagon/cart to carry your purchases back to your car. Free and open to the public. Registration not required.  MORE INFO.

item.title: Stifel Stars on Ice Pre-Show Stargazer Pass
item.description:
Saturday, May 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Climate Pledge Arena, 334 1st Ave N, Seattle, WA

THIS IS AN OPTIONAL UPGRADE. TICKET TO THE MAIN EVENT IS ALSO REQUIRED. Pre-Show Stargazer Event takes place prior to Stars on Ice show. Enjoy early entry, watch the skater warm-ups, and participate in a Q&A with several skaters from specified seating sections. Due to this event taking place prior to doors opening, EVERYONE entering the venue early must have an event ticket AND Pre-Show Stargazer Pass for entry. This includes all children as well as the parent(s)/guardian(s) wishing to accompany them into the building. If you plan on attending the Pre-Show Stargazer Event, please make sure you have BOTH tickets. NO EXCEPTIONS. Late arrivals will not be refunded. OPTIONAL UPSELL. NOT VALID FOR EVENT ADMISSION. MUST ALSO HAVE EVENT TICKET! FAN MUST BRING UPSELL TICKET TO EVENT DAY FOR ENTRY. NOTE SPECIFIC PRE-SHOW ACCESS START TIME! STARGAZER PASS TICKET REQUIRED FOR ENTRY.

From Climate Pledge Arena

Plan your visit to Lumen Field and make it the best, most efficient time using the information regarding the stadium - https://www.lumenfield.com/plan-your-visit/stadium-guide This event is Mobile Only
